meta.name: Sri Lanka
meta.title: Sri Lanka Tour Package from Malaysia | Dhesu Travel & Tours
meta.description: From ancient kingdoms to misty tea hills and wildlife safaris, explore Sri Lanka with Dhesu. Trusted Malaysian travel agency since 1988. Group, custom and honeymoon packages available.
meta.targetKeyword: Sri Lanka tour package from Malaysia

hero.badge: The Pearl of the Indian Ocean
hero.headline: Sri Lanka Fits
hero.body: Ancient cities and sacred temples in the morning, mist-wrapped tea hills in the afternoon, and leopards in the national park at dusk. Sri Lanka is compact enough to do it all, and rich enough that you will never feel like you have.

stats.label: Years Sending Malaysians to Sri Lanka

why.heading: Small Island, Impossible Variety
why.subheading: Most visitors are surprised by how much fits into one island. Ancient ruins, mountain trains, wildlife safaris, colonial beaches, and some of the best tea on earth, all within a few hours of each other.

highlights.title: Kandy: The Last Royal Capital
highlights.body: Kandy is Sri Lanka's cultural heartbeat, a UNESCO World Heritage city cradled in misty hills around a lake. The Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic draws pilgrims from across the Buddhist world. In the evenings, traditional Kandyan dancers and drummers perform a show that has been running for centuries.

highlights.title: Nuwara Eliya: Little England in the Clouds
highlights.body: At 1,868 metres above sea level, Nuwara Eliya is refreshingly cool and draped in tea estates that roll over every hillside. Colonial bungalows, rose gardens, and the rack railway through the Ella Gap make this the most cinematic corner of the island.

highlights.title: Sigiriya: The Rock Fortress in the Sky
highlights.body: Rising 200 metres from the jungle floor, Sigiriya is one of the most dramatic archaeological sites in Asia. A 5th-century king built his palace on top, with frescoes, mirror walls and water gardens that survive today. The climb rewards you with a view that stretches to the horizon in every direction.

highlights.title: Yala: Leopards and Elephants at Close Range
highlights.body: Yala National Park has one of the highest densities of leopards in the world. Jeep safaris at dawn or dusk regularly turn up herds of elephants, crocodiles on the riverbanks, and peacocks stepping through the scrub. The south coast beaches are minutes away from the park gate.

zones.heading: Where to Go and Why
zones.subheading: Sri Lanka is small enough to combine destinations in a single trip. Each place has a completely different character.

areas.name: Kandy
areas.description: The last royal capital of the ancient kings, set around a sacred lake with the Temple of the Tooth Relic at its heart. The cultural heartbeat of Sri Lanka.

areas.name: Nuwara Eliya
areas.description: Cool mountain air at 1,900 metres, endless tea estates and the scenic railway between Kandy and Ella. Sri Lanka's Little England.

areas.name: Colombo
areas.description: A layered capital of Dutch colonial quarters, Buddhist temples, spice bazaars and a waterfront promenade on the Indian Ocean. Every Sri Lanka trip begins here.

areas.name: Yala National Park
areas.description: The world's highest density of wild leopards. Dawn jeep safaris through the scrub forest regularly turn up elephants, sloth bears and crocodiles.

areas.name: Jaffna
areas.description: The Tamil heartland of Sri Lanka's north — Dutch Fort, Nallur Kovil and the sacred Nainativu Island. A culture closer to Tamil Nadu than Colombo.

areas.name: Sigiriya
areas.description: A 5th-century palace on a 200-metre granite monolith with ancient frescoes and water gardens. One of the most dramatic archaeological sites in Asia.

areas.name: Kataragama
areas.description: One of Sri Lanka's holiest towns — revered by Buddhists, Hindus and Muslims alike, on the edge of Yala National Park.

bestTime.heading: Best Time to Visit Sri Lanka from Malaysia
bestTime.body: Sri Lanka has two monsoon seasons that affect different coasts, so the right time depends on where you plan to go.

seasons.name: West & South Coast Season
seasons.description: Dry and sunny on the west and south coasts, and in the Cultural Triangle. This is the prime window for Colombo, Kandy, Sigiriya, Nuwara Eliya and Yala. Ideal for first-timers.

seasons.name: East Coast & Hill Country
seasons.description: The east coast and Jaffna are dry from May through September while the west gets its monsoon. The hill country and Kandy are fine year-round with afternoon showers.

tripLength.heading: How Many Days Do You Actually Need?

options.label: One Region
options.description: Enough for Kandy and Colombo, or a focused cultural triangle escape. Good if you have visited before and want to go deeper in one area.

options.label: Sweet Spot
options.description: The right amount of time to combine culture, hill country and either the south coast or wildlife. Most first-timers do best with a week.

options.label: Full Circuit
options.description: Enough to cover north and south, from Jaffna to Yala, with time in the hills and the Cultural Triangle. The full island experience.

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Sri Lanka Trip Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Sri Lanka rewards travellers who know where to stay, when to arrive, and which roads to take. We have been arranging Sri Lanka journeys for Malaysians since 1988, and our ground team knows the difference between the rooms that face the lake and the ones that face the car park.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered

points.title: Trusted Since 1988
